data.change_summary: Sixteenth remediation round addressing 3 amendments: (1) Added signal-kill warning logging in classify_exit_failure: on Unix, when status.code() is None (process killed by signal), extracts the signal number via std::os::unix::process::ExitStatusExt::signal() and emits a tracing::warn with the signal number for operator diagnosis. Retry behavior unchanged (TransportFailure). The match was refactored into three explicit arms: Some(127) → BinaryNotFound, Some(_) → TransportFailure, None → warn + TransportFailure. (2) Changed the pre-backoff snapshot write from `let _ = run_snapshot_write.write_run_snapshot(...)` to propagate the error via `?`. If the write fails, the run aborts immediately instead of silently continuing into a backoff window that cannot be crash-recovered. This matches the pattern used in fail_run() at engine.rs:5093. (3) Fixed derive_resume_state() to preserve retry-attempt state across crash: added a HashMap tracking the highest retryable-failed attempt per (stage_id, cycle) from StageFailed journal events with will_retry=true. The resume cursor now starts at failed_attempt+1 instead of hardcoded 1, preventing retry budget replenishment when the process is killed during the backoff window.
outstanding_risks: Process group kill cannot be implemented due to #![forbid(unsafe_code)] — pre_exec(setsid) requires unsafe, and std::process::Command::process_group is nightly-only. Grandchildren spawned by claude/codex CLI may survive as orphans after the parent is killed on timeout/cancel.

The signal warning in classify_exit_failure uses cfg(unix) gating. On non-Unix platforms the None arm still returns TransportFailure without any warning logging. This is acceptable since non-Unix platforms (Windows) use different process termination semantics (TerminateProcess) that don't involve signals.

The pre-backoff snapshot write now propagates errors via ?, which aborts the entire run if the write fails. The snapshot has already been mutated (status=Failed, active_run=None) in memory but won't reach disk. The error propagates to the caller, and the on-disk snapshot remains in the previous Running state. This is the correct behavior — the operator sees the write error rather than the run silently entering an unrecoverable state.

The derive_resume_state attempt tracking only considers StageFailed events with will_retry=true. Terminal failures (will_retry=false) don't contribute to the attempt counter because a terminal failure means the run is finished, not mid-retry. If the same stage+cycle has both retryable and terminal failures (unlikely but possible with mixed failure classes), only the retryable attempts count toward the resume budget.
The BinaryNotFound failure class name is used for both missing binaries and missing API keys. Doc comments clarify this dual use as a general 'fatal infrastructure prerequisite' signal.

steps.description: Added signal-kill warning logging in classify_exit_failure (process_backend.rs:1496-1520): refactored the match from two arms (Some(127), _) to three explicit arms (Some(127) → BinaryNotFound, Some(_) → TransportFailure, None → warn + TransportFailure). The None arm uses cfg(unix) to import ExitStatusExt and call status.signal(), emitting a tracing::warn with the signal number. Updated doc comment to describe the warning behavior.

Changed pre-backoff snapshot write from `let _ = run_snapshot_write.write_run_snapshot(base_dir, project_id, snapshot)` to `run_snapshot_write.write_run_snapshot(base_dir, project_id, snapshot)?` at engine.rs:4643. If the write fails, the error propagates immediately instead of silently continuing into a backoff window that leaves the project stranded in Running state on crash.

Added retryable_failed_attempts HashMap<(StageId, u32), u32> tracking in derive_resume_state (engine.rs:5796-5828): new StageFailed match arm extracts stage_id, cycle, and attempt from the event details, and updates the max attempt when will_retry is true. After the event loop, the resume cursor uses retryable_failed_attempts.get(&(resume_stage_id, resume_cycle)).map(|a| a + 1).unwrap_or(1) instead of hardcoded 1, preserving the retry budget across crash-during-backoff restarts.
Verified all 683 tests pass, cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean, cargo fmt --check clean.

validation_evidence: cargo test: 683 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored across all test binaries
cargo clippy: no warnings or errors (Checking ralph-burning → Finished dev profile)
cargo fmt --check: no formatting differences (exit 0, no output)

classify_exit_failure now has three explicit match arms: Some(127) → BinaryNotFound, Some(_) → TransportFailure, None → tracing::warn(signal) + TransportFailure. The cfg(unix) block imports ExitStatusExt at the call site, keeping the import scoped to the platform-specific code path.
Pre-backoff snapshot write at engine.rs:4643 now uses ? operator matching the pattern in fail_run() at engine.rs:5093. Both sites propagate write errors as AppResult failures.

derive_resume_state correctly tracks retryable failures: StageFailed events with will_retry=true update the per-(stage_id, cycle) max attempt. The resume cursor starts at max_attempt+1, so a crash during backoff after attempt 3 resumes at attempt 4 instead of resetting to 1.

Existing retry_success_on_second_attempt and retry_exhaustion tests exercise the backoff snapshot round-trip and would detect regressions in the active_run restore path. The derive_resume_state change is exercised by resume-from-failed tests which replay journal events to reconstruct the cursor.
All 72 workflow_engine_test tests pass with --features test-stub: retry exhaustion, resume-from-failed, cancellation during backoff, and preflight failure tests are all unaffected

All process_backend_test, tmux_adapter_test, and retry_policy_test tests pass — the classify_exit_failure refactor preserves the same classification behavior for all exit codes while adding the signal warning for the None (signal-killed) path
